title: Dna Replication
definition: The process by which a cell duplicates its genome prior to cell division.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The copying of a cell's genetic material (DNA) into two identical sets before the cell divides into two daughter cells.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A key process in cell division where the cell's genetic information is duplicated, ensuring that each daughter cell receives an identical set of genes.
difficulty_levels.expert: The fundamental process by which a cell replicates its genome prior to division, shedding light on the mechanisms of inheritance and mutations, and providing insights into cancer development and gene therapy.

extra_fields.insight: The intricate dance of enzymes and base-pairing rules that ensure genetic information is faithfully copied during cell division.
